items.description: Andy Burnham, former Greater Manchester mayor and newly elected Labour MP, is the seventh Prime Minister in a decade.

items.title: Stories that caught our eye: July 10 to 17
items.description: A selection of stories that caught our eye over the last seven days in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Two French firefighters die tackling blaze near Bordeaux airport
items.description: The blaze close to the parking lots at Bordeaux-Mérignac Airport is said to have originated from a brush fire.

items.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires military chief after days of protests
items.description: Mass protests in recent days in Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities had called for the removal of commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi. Volodymyr Zelenskyy has named Mykhailo Drapatyi as the army's new chief.

items.title: Tesla expands robotaxi service to Orlando, Tampa ahead of earnings
India's 'cockroach' protest continues a day after police crackdown
items.description: Delhi witnessed overwhelming violence on Monday, with at least 60 protesters wounded. Police said 118 of their men were also injured.

items.title: China narrows AI gap, challenges US influence
items.description: At the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai, Beijing showcased advances in AI while promoting its vision of open-source development and global governance.

items.title: Why the youth jobs crisis is becoming global
items.description: Young people in much of the world face growing competition for entry-level jobs as graduate numbers rise, hiring slows and AI-driven disruption adds uncertainty to career prospects.

items.title: Master and apprentice as Spain, Argentina coaches meet in World Cup final
items.description: Nine years ago, Argentina's coach was just another former professional player pursuing a career in coaching, studying for his pro-licence on the outskirts of Madrid. His teacher on the course? Luis de la Fuente. Leer más
